checks.finding: Design Architecture explains that explicit feature mode preserves legacy and LITE validity; Architecture Decision Records explains why no new ADR is needed; Claude Design Integration and Review-Loop Compatibility justify retaining Mermaid and extending existing gates.
design.md ## Open Questions states None; no unresolved question requires ownership, blocking classification, or a resolution path.
design.md ## Assumptions states None and identifies the paired-script fixture location and synchronized 1.4.0 policy as repository-backed decisions recorded in requirements.md.

design.md ## Constraint Compliance addresses LITE preservation, legacy compatibility, safe feature-slug validation, Bash/PowerShell parity, visualization limits, review order, and preservation of user changes; no requirements constraint is contradicted.

REQ-007's frontend performance budgets and numeric infrastructure SLO requirements are represented in design.md ## Layer Artifact Model and Cross-Layer Dependencies, while ## Test Strategy requires static assertions for layer templates and acceptance-linked SLO coverage.

design.md ## Deployment / CI Plan states that there is no runtime deployment and identifies repository validation and CI as consumers of updated tests and synchronized metadata. ## Data Plan states that no database migration is required, so rollout flags and migration ordering are not applicable.
design.md ## Data Plan declares no database entities and explicitly states that no database migration is required; existing documents remain valid in repository mode.

No external service or cross-service call is introduced. design.md ## Claude Design Integration explicitly limits visualization to an optional manual workflow and disclaims direct Figma API access, bidirectional synchronization, and automatic image analysis.

The design covers the four layer artifacts, cross-layer index and traceability, bilingual question bank, visualization guide, explicit checker mode, LITE and legacy compatibility, parity, release metadata, extended existing review gates, and approval boundary required by REQ-006 through REQ-017; no unsupported product component, endpoint, or data model is introduced.

design.md ## Test Strategy names concrete static, integration, acceptance, parity, tamper, path-substitution, invalid-slug, traceability, and Draft-approval-boundary fixtures for the filesystem security boundary and changed CLI/review contracts.
